messages.content:
What type of attack occurs when the attacker spoofs the IP address of the victim, sending a continuous stream of small requests, which produce a continuous stream of much larger replies that are to be sent to the victim's IP address?
	• reflection attack
	• amplification attack
	• MITM attack
Trojan virus
The correct answer is: amplification attack

A ping attack that exploits the broadcast IP address in a subnet is referred to as what type of attack?
	• red rat
	• fraggle
	• smurf
	• SYN flood
firewalk
The correct answer is: smurf

What best describes an attack surface?
	• a way to classify which tools were used in an attack
	• the sum of the different points (attack vectors) in a given computing device or network that are accessible to an unauthorized user (attacker)
	• the people who are involved in protecting the network perimeter
only describes the data that is gathered about an attack
The correct answer is: the sum of the different points (attack vectors) in a given computing device or network that are accessible to an unauthorized user (attacker)

What type of attack occurs when an attacker sends a flood of protocol request packets to various IP hosts and the attacker spoofs the source IP address of the packets, such that each packet has the IP address of the intended target rather than the IP address of the attacker as its source address?
	• reflection attack
	• amplification attack
	• MITM attack
Trojan virus ARP
The correct answer is: reflection attack

What is an example of a reconnaissance attack tool that will cycle through all well-known ports to provide a complete list of all services that are running on the hosts?
	• Netuse
	• ipconfig
	• NMAP
show run
The correct answer is: NMAP

Which two of the following statements are true regarding early TCP/IP development? (Choose two.)
	• TCP/IP was the only network protocol suite available and was developed for internet work environments.
	• The focus was on solving the technical challenges of moving information quickly and reliably, not to secure it.
	• The model was developed as a flexible, fault-tolerant set of protocols.
The design and architecture of TCP/IP have not changed since its adoption in the early 1970s.

The two true statements regarding early TCP/IP development are:
- The focus was on solving the technical challenges of moving information quickly and reliably, not to secure it.
- The model was developed as a flexible, fault-tolerant set of protocols.

What type of spoofing attack uses fake source IP addresses that are different than their real IP addresses?
	• MAC spoofing
	• IP spoofing
	• application spoofing
name spoofing
The correct answer is: IP spoofing

Which two attacks can be caused by a rogue DHCP server? (Choose two.)
	• Trojan virus
	• Compromised-Key
	• DoS
	• TCP SYN flood
MITM

The two attacks that can be caused by a rogue DHCP server are:
- DoS (Denial of Service)
- MITM (Man-in-the-Middle)

What best describes an attack vector?
	• the resolution of an attack
	• a path, method, or route by which an attack was carried out
	• the result of, or damage from, an attack
the last stage of the attack continuum
The correct answer is: a path, method, or route by which an attack was carried out

What best describes a DoS attack?
	• attempts to consume all of a critical computer or network resource in order to make it unavailable for valid use
	• poses as legitimate software or email attachment in order to launch a malicious attack when opened
	• can steal data such as user names and passwords without the user realizing that they have been compromised
rarely seen because DoS attacks are extremely difficult to engineer and almost impossible to deliver
The correct answer is: attempts to consume all of a critical computer or network resource in order to make it unavailable for valid use

Which two are software vulnerability scanners? (Choose two.)
	• VmStat
	• Nessus
	• fingerprint
open VAS

The two software vulnerability scanners are:
- Nessus
- OpenVAS

Question 27
Which statement is correct about the Cisco Secure Firewall Threat Defense IPS functionality?
	• Can be deployed as as IDS or IPS.
	• Can drop malicious packets when deployed in IDS mode.
	• Can only be deployed inline.
Can analyze traffic only at layer 3 and layer

The correct statement about the Cisco Secure Firewall Threat Defense IPS functionality is: 

- Can be deployed as as IDS or IPS.

Which Cisco Umbrella feature provides content filtering by category or specific URLs to block destinations that violate policies or compliance regulations?
	• DNS Layer Security (DLS)
	• Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB)
	• Secure Web Gateway (SWG)
	• Cloud Delivered Firewall (CDFW)
Applications Firewall (AFW)
The correct answer is: Secure Web Gateway (SWG)

You are currently configuring and tuning a new IPS on your development network. You have confirmed that traffic to and from the internet is being inspected by the IPS, but traffic between the local LAN segments are not being inspected by the IPS. What could be the problem?
	• You neglected to enable some of the Snort rules.
	• You neglected to enable anomaly-based processing.
	• You placed the IPS on a network segment that has no access to the traffic between the local LAN segments.
You placed the IPS in detection mode instead of prevention mode.
The correct answer is: You placed the IPS on a network segment that has no access to the traffic between the local LAN segments.

Which two of the following are true statements about the Snort detection system? (Choose two.)
	• Source code became proprietary after the Cisco acquisition.
	• It is an anomaly-based intrusion system.
	• The Base Ruleset is updated automatically and in real-time.
	• Snort is a signature-based intrusion detection system.
The NIDS mode of operation is the only mode that provides intrusion prevention functionality.

The two true statements about the Snort detection system are:
- Snort is a signature-based intrusion detection system.
- The NIDS mode of operation is the only mode that provides intrusion prevention functionality.

Which three of the followings are major categories of Snort rule options? (Choose three.)
	• General
	• Payload
	• Protocol
	• Operator
Postdetectio

The three major categories of Snort rule options are:
- General
- Payload
- Protocol

What is checked when the Snort engine starts up?
	• Snort log files for any Snort processes errors.
	• Syntax of all the Snort rules in the Snort rules file.
	• Version of the Snort rules.
Snort license is valid.
The correct answer is: Syntax of all the Snort rules in the Snort rules file.

What is checked when the Snort engine starts up?
	• Snort log files for any Snort processes errors.
	• Syntax of all the Snort rules in the Snort rules file.
	• Version of the Snort rules.
Snort license is valid.
The correct answer is: Snort log files for any Snort processes errors.

Which statement is true regarding Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall?
	• Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall is supported by constructing a GRE tunnel between the on-premise network device and the Cisco Umbrella cloud.
	• Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall is supported by constructing an IPsec tunnel between the on-premise network device and the Cisco Umbrella cloud.
	• Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall only supports layer 3/4 inspections.
Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall requires the Umbrella Roaming Clients.

The correct statement regarding Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall is: 

- Cisco Cloud-Delivered Firewall requires the Umbrella Roaming Clients.

Which three options are tools that can perform packet captures? (Choose three.)
	• Wireshark
	• ELSA
	• Sguil
	• Squert
	• Tshark
tcpdump

The three tools that can perform packet captures are:

- Wireshark
- Tshark
- tcpdump

According to the following figure, which three statements are true? (Choose three.)

	• The destination port is associated with the HTTPS protocol.
	• The source and destination IP addresses are private IP addresses.
	• The attacker uses random source ports.
	• The attack targets the same web server.
The alerts indicate an SQL injection attack.
Without being able to see the figure provided, I cannot accurately determine which statements are true. Please provide more information or the figure itself to assist with the analysis.

The Cisco SecureX platform does not integrate with which part of an organization's network?
	• endpoints
	• network traffic
	• optical transceivers
	• data centers
cloud-based applications
The correct answer is: optical transceivers

Match the example NSM data to the associated NSM data type.
extracted content
HTTP throughput baseline
full packet capture
PDF file
metadata
reputation
transaction data
DNS query and response
statistical data
PCAP file

The matching of the example NSM data to the associated NSM data type is as follows:

1. Extracted content - PDF file
2. HTTP throughput baseline - Statistical data
3. Full packet capture - PCAP file
4. PDF file - Extracted content
5. Metadata - DNS query and response
6. Reputation - Metadata
7. Transaction data - DNS query and response
8. DNS query and response - Transaction data
9. Statistical data - HTTP throughput baseline
10. PCAP file - Full packet capture

Match the example NSM data to the associated NSM data type.
extracted content
HTTP throughput baseline
full packet capture
PDF file
metadata
reputation
transaction data
DNS query and response
statistical data
PCAP file

The matching of the example NSM data to the associated NSM data type is as follows:

1. Extracted content - Unstructured
2. HTTP throughput baseline - Statistical
3. Full packet capture - Packets
4. PDF file - Unstructured
5. Metadata - Contextual
6. Reputation - Contextual
7. Transaction data - Structured
8. DNS query and response - Packets and Contextual
9. Statistical data - Contextual
10. PCAP file - Packets

Regarding the following figure, which two statements are true? (Choose two.)

	• Tools such as OSSEC, Bro, and syslog-ng produce flat files with one log entry per line and are largely dedicated to collecting and producing raw NSM data.
	• Tools such as PCAP, Sguil, and ELSA DB produce flat files with one log entry per line that are largely dedicated to collecting and producing raw NSM data.
	• Components such as Sguil DB and ELSA are associated with optimizing and maintaining.
	• The tools in the top row are associated with optimizing and maintaining the data.
Alert data must also include the metadata that is associated with the IPS alert.

Based on the given information, the two true statements are:

- Tools such as OSSEC, Bro, and syslog-ng produce flat files with one log entry per line and are largely dedicated to collecting and producing raw NSM data.
- The tools in the top row are associated with optimizing and maintaining the data.

Match the example NSM data to the associated NSM data type.
extracted content
HTTP throughput baseline
full packet capture
PDF file
metadata
reputation
transaction data
DNS query and response
statistical data
PCAP file

The matching of the example NSM data to the associated NSM data type is as follows:

1. Extracted content - Unstructured
2. HTTP throughput baseline - Statistical
3. Full packet capture - Packets
4. PDF file - Unstructured
5. Metadata - Contextual
6. Reputation - Contextual
7. Transaction data - Structured
8. DNS query and response - Packets and Contextual
9. Statistical data - Contextual
10. PCAP file - Raw Packets

Which method allows you to verify entity authentication, data integrity, and authenticity of communications, without encrypting the actual data?
	• Both parties calculate an authenticated MD5 hash value of the data accompanying the message—one party uses the private key, while the other party uses the public key.
	• Both parties to the communication use the same secret key to produce a message authentication code to accompany the message.
	• Both parties calculate a CRC32 of the data before and after transmission of the message.
Both parties obfuscate the data with XOR and a known key before and after transmission of the message.
The correct answer is: Both parties to the communication use the same secret key to produce a message authentication code to accompany the message.
